subject: Geography
title: Mexico City: Built on a Lake
description: A micro-lesson that makes Mexico City: Built on a Lake usable.

hook.text: Mexico City was built on top of a lake that the Spanish conquerors drained 500 years ago.

buildup.text: The Aztec capital Tenochtitlan was a lake city with causeways, canals, and floating gardens.

discovery.text: Today, 22 million people live on the soft lakebed. The city sinks 50 cm per year in places.

twist.text: Buildings lean visibly. The grand cathedral has sunk meters below its original street level.

climax.text: A city built by draining a lake now faces catastrophic water shortages.

punchline.text: They drained the lake. Now they can't find enough water.

quiz.question: What was Mexico City built on?
